meta.feed_title: Latest World News on Fox News

items.title: Zelenskyy offers cutting-edge drone defense to Gulf allies as Ukraine seeks missile support
items.summary: Ukraine's president offered the country's counter-drone technology to Gulf nations, seeking missile support as Ukraine faces a critical air defense deficit.

items.title: Body found in search for missing American Airlines flight attendant in Colombia: mayor
items.summary: After American Eric Fernando Gutierrez Molina went missing, a mayor in Colombia noted the discovery of a corpse that he said likely is that of the missing man.

items.title: Iran-backed Houthis open third front against Israel as Tehran seeks leverage ahead of talks
items.summary: Houthis launched ballistic and cruise missiles at Israel, which says it intercepted the strike. Analysts say Iran is pressuring the Houthis to show leverage in U.S. talks.

items.title: Ugandan military chief vows to back Israel against Iran in viral social media barrage
items.summary: Uganda's military chief Gen. Muhoozi Kainerugaba claims Uganda could enter the Iran-Israel war on Israel's side, posting a series of pro-Israel statements on X this week.

items.title: Inside Iran’s military: missiles, militias and a force built for survival
items.summary: Experts say Iran's military is built to survive, not win conventionally, as the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps retains missiles, proxies and naval threats despite U.S. strikes.

items.title: The race against time to destroy Iran’s illicit nuclear weapons program heats up amid fresh strikes
items.summary: The IDF says it struck Iran's Arak heavy water plant, a key plutonium site, as experts warn Natanz and Isfahan still hold highly enriched uranium.

items.title: Russian man who assaulted woman during Barron Trump FaceTime call sentenced to 4 years
items.summary: Russian MMA fighter Matvei Rumiantsev was sentenced to four years for assaulting a woman in London in an attack Barron Trump witnessed on FaceTime.

items.title: Rubio meets G7 ministers in France as US leads on Iran — allies under fire for tepid response
items.summary: Secretary of State Marco Rubio heads into the G7 foreign ministers meeting in France saying U.S. priorities come first, as European allies push for a diplomatic solution on Iran.

items.title: Zelenskyy claims US tied Ukraine security guarantees to giving up Donbas, White House denies
items.summary: Zelenskyy claimed U.S. security guarantees for Ukraine are tied to ceding Donbas to Russia, but the White House says those claims are false.

items.title: Israel says Iranian leader who ordered Strait of Hormuz closure killed in targeted strike
items.summary: Israel has eliminated Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps Navy Commander Alireza Tangsiri in a strike, Israel's Minister of Defense Israel Katz indicated.

items.title: Over 90% of Iranian missiles intercepted, but a critical vulnerability is growing, report warns
items.summary: Experts say Iran's cheap drones and missiles are depleting allied interceptor stockpiles, raising questions about long-term air defense sustainability.

items.title: US troops brace for ‘hit-and-run’ guerilla attacks as 82nd Airborne deploys to Iran, military analyst warns
items.summary: The 82nd Airborne Division deployment to the Middle East is intended to pressure Iran into accepting U.S. ceasefire terms, military analyst Michael Eisenstadt says.

items.title: Meet Iran's hardline speaker who threatened to burn US forces — reportedly Tehran's point man for talks
items.summary: Iranian parliament speaker Ghalibaf has threatened U.S. forces while reportedly being eyed by the Trump administration as a potential Iran talks channel.

items.title: Iran-linked influence campaign pushes anti-Israel messaging disguised as US voices: report
items.summary: Foreign accounts allegedly mimic American voices online, with Argyle research claiming 60% of Operation Epic Fury discourse originates outside the U.S.

items.title: UK arrests 2 over 'antisemitic arson attack' as police investigate possible Iran link
items.summary: U.K. police arrested two men allegedly behind an antisemitic arson attack on Jewish ambulances in London that Prime Minister Keir Starmer called "horrifying."

items.title: US strikes against Iran-backed militias in Iraq reportedly continue as Baghdad warns of 'right to respond'
items.summary: Iraq's Prime Minister al-Sudani threatened to confront and respond to U.S. airstrikes on PMF headquarters, calling the attacks violations of Iraqi sovereignty.

items.title: Who actually runs Iran right now? The key power players as Trump claims talks to 'top' official
items.summary: Analysts say the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps has emerged as Iran's dominant force, with Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei's grip on power reportedly uncertain after recent strikes.

items.title: US military sends drones, alongside 200 troops, to Nigeria amid fears of renewed Boko Haram insurgency
items.summary: The U.S. military deployed MQ-9 Reaper drones to Nigeria for intelligence-gathering and training as the African country is grappling with a security crisis.

items.title: North Korean dictator says government will keep cementing nation's 'irreversible status as a nuclear power'
items.summary: North Korean figure Kim Jong Un said the government will bolster the nation's "irreversible status as a nuclear power," The Associated Press reported.

items.title: NATO scrambles jets as Russia fires nearly 400 drones toward Ukraine, signaling new spring offensive
items.summary: Russia launched nearly 400 drones and missiles at Ukraine, prompting Poland and Romania to scramble NATO fighter jets as strikes neared their airspace.

items.title: Defiant Iran vows to fight 'until complete victory,' despite heavy military losses
items.summary: An Iranian military spokesman said Tuesday that Tehran's armed forces will battle "until complete victory," despite the U.S. reporting heavy losses for them.

items.title: Colombian military plane crash kills at least 66, head of armed forces says
items.summary: A Colombian military transport plane crashed near Puerto Leguizamo, Colombia, shortly after takeoff, killing at least 66 people and injuring dozens.

items.title: 'Americas Counter Cartel Coalition': Inside the US strategy to combat narco terror, confront China, other foes
items.summary: Joseph M. Humire, acting assistant secretary of War for Homeland Defense and Americas Security Affairs, explains the Trump administration's strategy for the Western Hemisphere.

items.title: Zelenskyy says Ukraine has evidence Russia is aiding Iran with intelligence
items.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy, whose nation has been at war with Russia for years, has indicated that there is evidence that Russia is supplying Iran with intelligence.

items.title: Iran conflict tests Pakistan amid own border clashes as Islamabad touted as venue for US-Tehran talks
items.summary: Pakistan, the only nuclear-armed Muslim state, faces mounting pressure as its Saudi defense pact and Iran ties clash amid the intensifying regional war.
